name: Costa Rican Casado
steps: Grill or pan-sear the chicken breast until cooked through, then let rest and slice.
Fry the plantain slices until golden on both sides.
Plate the rice, beans, plantain, cabbage, and sliced chicken together.
steps_detailed: Season the chicken breast with black pepper. Heat a splash of the vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and cook the chicken 5-6 minutes per side until cooked through and lightly charred. Rest 5 minutes, then slice.
In the same skillet, fry the plantain slices in the remaining oil, 2-3 minutes per side, until golden and caramelized at the edges.
Toss the cabbage and carrot with the lime juice for a quick slaw.
Plate the rice and black beans side by side, then arrange the sliced chicken, fried plantain, and slaw around them - the classic composed casado plate.
description: Casado, meaning 'married', a plate combining rice, beans, meat, and salad, is Costa Rica's most iconic everyday meal.

name: Costa Rican Gallo Pinto
steps: Saute the onion until soft.
Add the cooked rice, black beans, and Worcestershire sauce.
Fry together over medium-high heat, stirring often, until well combined and lightly toasted.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion, bell pepper, and garlic in the vegetable oil over medium heat for 4-5 minutes until softened.
Add the black beans with a splash of their liquid and simmer 3-4 minutes to let the flavors meld.
Add the rice and Worcestershire sauce, and fry everything together over medium-high heat for 6-8 minutes, stirring and pressing frequently, until evenly colored and a little crisp in spots.
Stir in the cilantro just before serving.
description: Gallo pinto, rice and beans cooked together, is considered Costa Rica's national dish.

name: Costa Rican Picadillo de Chayote
steps: Brown the ground beef with the onion until cooked through.
Add the diced chayote and tomatoes, cover, and simmer 12-15 minutes until the chayote is tender.
Season to taste and serve.
steps_detailed: Brown the ground beef with the onion, bell pepper, and garlic over medium-high heat, 6-7 minutes, until cooked through.
Stir in the diced chayote, diced tomatoes, and cumin.
Cover and simmer over medium-low heat 12-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the chayote is tender but still holds its shape.
Uncover and simmer 3-4 more minutes to thicken slightly if there's excess liquid.
Stir in the cilantro just before serving.
description: Picadillo, a category of minced vegetable-and-meat side dishes, this version built on chayote squash.

name: Costa Rican Gallo Pinto with Eggs and Natilla
steps: Saute the onion, then add the rice and beans, stir-frying 5-6 minutes until well combined.
Fry the egg separately to your liking.
Top with cilantro and serve with a generous dollop of natilla (Costa Rican sour cream) - a classic, hearty Costa Rican breakfast.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion, then add the rice and beans, stir-frying 5-6 minutes until well combined.
Fry the egg separately to your liking.
Top with cilantro and serve with a generous dollop of natilla (Costa Rican sour cream) - a classic, hearty Costa Rican breakfast.
Sour cream is thinner and tangier than cream cheese, closer to the real consistency of Latin American crema or natilla, which pours rather than spreads.
description: Gallo pinto with eggs and natilla, a heartier version of Costa Rica's national dish for breakfast.

name: Costa Rican Chorreadas

ingredients.ingredient: Corn, sweet, yellow, canned, drained solids, rinsed with tap water

ingredients.note: as a natilla stand-in, to serve

steps: Blend the corn with the flour and sugar into a thick, slightly chunky batter.
Cook spoonfuls on a hot, lightly greased griddle, 3-5 minutes per side, until golden with lightly crisp edges.
Serve with a dollop of natilla - chorreadas, fresh corn pancakes, are a beloved Costa Rican breakfast.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Corn, sweet, yellow, canned, drained solids, rinsed with tap water

ingredients_full.note: as a natilla stand-in, to serve

steps_detailed: Blend the corn with the flour and sugar into a thick, slightly chunky batter.
Cook spoonfuls on a hot, lightly greased griddle, 3-5 minutes per side, until golden with lightly crisp edges.
Serve with a dollop of natilla - chorreadas, fresh corn pancakes, are a beloved Costa Rican breakfast.
Sour cream is thinner and tangier than cream cheese, closer to the real consistency of Latin American crema or natilla, which pours rather than spreads.
description: Chorreadas, sweet corn pancakes, a beloved Costa Rican specialty.

name: Costa Rican Fried Plantains with Beans

ingredients.ingredient: Frijoles rojos volteados (Refried beans, red, canned)

steps: Fry the plantain slices until golden.
Warm the refried beans.
Serve together with the queso fresco.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Frijoles rojos volteados (Refried beans, red, canned)

steps_detailed: Fry the plantain slices in the oil over medium heat, 3-5 minutes per side, until deeply golden.
Warm the refried beans.
Serve everything together with the crumbled queso fresco - a comforting Costa Rican breakfast plate.
description: Plantains and beans, a simple, classic Costa Rican pairing.

name: Costa Rican Chifrijo
steps: Layer the rice and beans in a bowl.
Top with the crispy fried pork and diced tomatoes.
Serve with tortilla chips.
steps_detailed: Fry the pork cubes until deeply browned and crisp on the edges, 8-10 minutes.
Layer the rice and beans in a bowl.
Mix the tomatoes, onion, cilantro, and lime juice into a fresh pico de gallo.
Top the rice and beans with the crispy pork and pico de gallo, and serve with tortilla chips - chifrijo, a beloved bar food, was invented in a San José bar and has become a Costa Rican icon.
description: Chifrijo, rice, beans, and crispy pork combined in one bowl, a modern Costa Rican bar-food invention.

name: Costa Rican Sopa Negra
steps: Simmer the beans with the onion and garlic in their liquid plus a splash of water for 15 minutes.
Blend partially for a thick, dark broth.
Top with the sliced egg and serve.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion, garlic, and bell pepper until softened, then add the beans with their liquid and a splash of water.
Simmer 18-22 minutes, then blend partially for a thick, dark, rustic broth.
Top with the sliced hard-boiled egg and cilantro - sopa negra, black bean soup with egg, is a Costa Rican comfort classic.
description: Sopa negra, a black bean soup with a poached egg, a genuine Costa Rican comfort food.

name: Costa Rican Olla de Carne

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Simmer the beef in water until nearly tender, about 45 minutes.
Add the chayote, potato, and carrot, and simmer 20 more minutes.
Serve hot with rice.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Simmer the beef with the onion in 3 cups water for 45-50 minutes until nearly tender.
Add the chayote, potato, and carrot, and simmer 20-25 more minutes until all the vegetables are tender.
Top with cilantro and serve hot with rice - olla de carne, a hearty mixed-vegetable beef soup, is a Costa Rican Sunday tradition.
description: Olla de carne, a hearty beef and vegetable soup, a Costa Rican Sunday tradition.

name: Costa Rican Arroz con Pollo
steps: Brown the chicken with the onion and bell pepper.
Add the rice and 1.25 cups water, and simmer, covered, 18-20 minutes until the rice is tender.
Serve hot.
steps_detailed: Brown the chicken with the onion, bell pepper, garlic, and turmeric over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the rice and 1.5 cups water, cover, and simmer 20-25 minutes until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Arroz con pollo, chicken and rice cooked together, is a beloved Costa Rican family dinner.
description: A simple chicken and rice dish, a Costa Rican home-cooking staple.

name: Costa Rican Tamales Costarricenses
steps: Whisk the masa harina with warm water into a thick dough.
Simmer the chicken with the potato and tomatoes until cooked; shred and mix into the filling.
Spread the dough on parchment or a soaked plantain leaf, top with the filling, fold into a packet, and steam 1 hour.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Whisk the masa harina with warm water and the oil into a thick, smooth dough.
Simmer the chicken with the potato and tomatoes for 20-25 minutes until cooked through; shred the chicken and fold in the raisins.
Spread a layer of the masa dough onto parchment or a soaked plantain leaf, top with the chicken filling, fold into a sealed packet.
Steam 1-1.25 hours until firm - Costa Rican tamales, traditionally wrapped in plantain leaf rather than corn husk, are a Christmas tradition.
description: Tamales, corn dough filled and wrapped in banana leaf, a genuine Costa Rican Christmas tradition.

name: Costa Rican Ceviche Costarricense
steps: Marinate the cubed fish in the lime juice for at least 20 minutes, until opaque and firm.
Stir in the onion and cilantro.
Serve chilled with soda crackers.
steps_detailed: Cube the fish and marinate in the lime juice for at least 25-30 minutes, until opaque and firm.
Stir in the onion, cilantro, tomatoes, and oil.
Chill briefly and serve with soda crackers - Costa Rican ceviche is typically milder and more citrus-forward than its spicier regional cousins.
description: Ceviche, citrus-cured raw fish, a Costa Rican coastal specialty.

name: Costa Rican Patacones
steps: Fry the plantain slices until just golden, then flatten each with the back of a spoon.
Fry again until crisp on both sides, and season with salt.
steps_detailed: Fry the plantain slices in the oil over medium heat, 2-3 minutes per side, until just golden.
Flatten each piece with the back of a spoon, then fry again, 2-3 minutes per side, until deeply golden and crisp.
Season with salt and a squeeze of lime - patacones, twice-fried plantains, are a beloved Costa Rican snack.
description: Patacones, twice-fried green plantains, a common Costa Rican side.

name: Costa Rican Arreglados
steps: Brown the beef.
Layer the phyllo with the melted butter, fill with the beef and cabbage, and fold into a packet.
Bake at 375F for 18-22 minutes until golden.
steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the tomatoes.
Layer sheets of phyllo, brushing with melted butter, then fill with the beef and cabbage and fold into a sealed packet.
Bake at 375F (190C) for 20-25 minutes until deeply golden and flaky - arreglados, savory puff pastry sandwiches, are a beloved Costa Rican bakery snack.
description: Arreglados, stuffed sandwich rolls, a beloved Costa Rican snack.

name: Costa Rican Empanadas de Costa Rica
steps: Mix the masa harina with warm water into a soft dough.
Fill with the shredded chicken, fold closed into half-moons, and fry until golden.
steps_detailed: Mix the masa harina with warm water into a soft, pliable dough.
Flatten portions, fill with the shredded chicken and queso fresco if using, and fold closed into half-moons, sealing well.
Fry in the oil, turning, 4-5 minutes until deeply golden - masa empanadas are a beloved Costa Rican snack, distinct from flour-based versions found elsewhere.
description: Empanadas, filled pastry pockets, a Costa Rican staple.

name: Costa Rican Tres Leches Cake
steps: Beat the eggs and sugar together until pale and thickened, 4-5 minutes.
Fold in the flour and vanilla gently until just combined.
Bake in a small buttered dish at 350F (175C) for 18-22 minutes until springy and golden. Let cool slightly.
Poke holes all over and pour the milk over, letting it soak in fully.
Top with a dollop of natilla before serving - tres leches cake is beloved throughout Costa Rica.
steps_detailed: Beat the eggs and sugar together until pale and thickened, 4-5 minutes.
Fold in the flour and vanilla gently until just combined.
Bake in a small buttered dish at 350F (175C) for 18-22 minutes until springy and golden. Let cool slightly.
Poke holes all over and pour the milk over, letting it soak in fully.
Top with a dollop of natilla before serving - tres leches cake is beloved throughout Costa Rica.
Sour cream is thinner and tangier than cream cheese, closer to the real consistency of Latin American crema or natilla, which pours rather than spreads.
description: Tres leches, a sponge cake soaked in three milks, a beloved dessert across Costa Rica and the wider region.

name: Costa Rican Churchill
steps: Layer shaved or crushed ice in a glass.
Top with the coconut milk, preserves, and a dollop of cream.
steps_detailed: Layer plenty of shaved or crushed ice in a tall glass.
Drizzle with the coconut milk and preserves, and top with a generous dollop of cream and a dusting of sugar.
Churchill, a festive shaved-ice dessert drink, is especially associated with Limón on Costa Rica's Caribbean coast.
description: Churchill, a shaved ice dessert with condensed milk and fruit, a genuine Costa Rican specialty named for Winston Churchill.

name: Costa Rican Yuca con Mojo
steps: Boil the cassava until tender, about 20 minutes.
Warm the garlic in the oil until fragrant, then stir in the lime juice.
Pour the garlic-lime oil over the cassava and serve.
steps_detailed: Boil the cassava until fully tender, 20-25 minutes, then drain.
Warm the garlic in the oil over low heat, 2-3 minutes, until fragrant but not browned, then stir in the lime juice and cilantro off the heat.
Pour the garlic-lime mojo generously over the warm cassava and serve - yuca con mojo is a beloved Costa Rican side and snack.
description: Yuca with a garlic-lime mojo, a common Costa Rican side.

name: Costa Rican Salsa Lizano Style
steps: Blend the tomatoes, vinegar, sugar, and paprika until smooth.
Serve alongside gallo pinto and casado.
steps_detailed: Blend the tomatoes, vinegar, sugar, paprika, and cumin until smooth.
This nods to Salsa Lizano, Costa Rica's iconic tangy-sweet condiment, inseparable from gallo pinto and casado.
description: Salsa Lizano, a tangy, slightly sweet condiment, is a genuine Costa Rican icon used on nearly everything.

name: Costa Rican Natilla
steps: Whisk the cream cheese with the milk and lime juice until smooth and pourable.
Serve alongside gallo pinto or fried plantains.
steps_detailed: Whisk the cream cheese with the milk and lime juice until smooth and pourable, thinning with a touch more milk if needed.
Natilla, Costa Rica's tangy soured cream, is drizzled over nearly everything from breakfast to desserts.
description: Natilla, a tangy, thick sour cream, a Costa Rican pantry staple.
